responses: We use an AI model to help our content team write product descriptions for our e-commerce platform. It generates draft descriptions that writers review and publish. The system uses a commercial API that we call from a simple internal tool the engineering team built.

We chose the model we use because it produces readable text and the pricing was acceptable for our volume. We briefly considered using open-source models but decided against it because we lacked the infrastructure expertise. No formal evaluation was done.

If the system fails, writers would produce descriptions manually, which would slow down catalog publishing. We have not thought carefully about edge cases or worst-case outputs. We rely on the writers to catch problems during their review step.

The main thing we learned is that AI-generated descriptions need a human review pass. We did not develop a systematic approach to catching AI errors beyond that. The team is generally satisfied with the quality but we have not measured it rigorously.

rationale: Tradeoff scores 2 because one alternative (open-source) is named, but no tradeoff analysis was conducted. All other dimensions are at 1. The purpose description is vague with no specific outcomes, and there is no failure mode or reflection depth.
